title: Fix a case without regressing an existing one
task: `joinNames` in src/joinNames.js should join names into an Oxford-comma phrase: [] -> "", ["a"] -> "a", ["a","b"] -> "a and b", ["a","b","c"] -> "a, b, and c", ["a","b","c","d"] -> "a, b, c, and d". The three-or-more case is currently wrong (plain comma join, no Oxford comma). Fix it so ALL cases pass — note the two-name case must keep its "a and b" form, which a naive rewrite focused only on 3+ tends to break. Do not modify the tests. Run the tests to verify.

checks.pattern: two names use 'and' with no comma

notes: Two pre-existing tests pull in opposite directions: a naive Oxford fix that forgets the two-name special case regresses it, and the original two-name code fails the 3+ case. Only a fix that satisfies both passes. Test pins block rewriting either assertion.
